title: The Comprehensive List of LLM Fine-Tuning Tools

tools.name: Axolotl
tools.description: Axolotl is a configuration-driven post-training framework supporting full fine-tuning, LoRA/QLoRA, preference optimization, reward modeling, GRPO-family methods, multimodal models, and distributed backends including FSDP and DeepSpeed.

tools.name: Unsloth
tools.description: Unsloth is a fine-tuning library with custom kernels and memory optimizations for SFT, LoRA/QLoRA, DPO, and reinforcement-learning workflows on supported language and vision-language models.

tools.name: LLaMA-Factory
tools.description: LLaMA-Factory is a unified fine-tuning framework with command-line and web interfaces for many text and multimodal models, covering pretraining, SFT, reward modeling, preference optimization, PPO, and adapter methods.

tools.name: NVIDIA NeMo Framework
tools.description: NVIDIA NeMo Framework is a modular framework for training, adapting, and evaluating language, speech, and multimodal models, with Megatron-based parallelism, PEFT recipes, checkpoint conversion, and NVIDIA platform integrations.

tools.name: Ludwig
tools.description: Ludwig is a declarative machine-learning framework that fine-tunes language models through YAML or Python configurations and includes data preprocessing, distributed training, hyperparameter search, evaluation, and model export.

tools.name: H2O LLM Studio
tools.description: H2O LLM Studio is a graphical and configuration-based application for fine-tuning instruction-following language models with LoRA, QLoRA, preference data, experiment comparison, and checkpoint export.

tools.name: ms-swift
tools.description: ms-swift is ModelScope's framework for training, aligning, evaluating, quantizing, and deploying text and multimodal models, with full-parameter, PEFT, Megatron, preference-learning, and GRPO-family recipes.

tools.name: XTuner
tools.description: XTuner is an open training engine for language and multimodal models that provides SFT, PEFT, preference optimization, reinforcement learning, sequence parallelism, and recipes for dense and mixture-of-experts architectures.

tools.name: AutoTrain Advanced
tools.description: AutoTrain Advanced is Hugging Face's low-code training toolkit for running text and multimodal fine-tuning jobs locally or on connected compute, with dataset preprocessing and Hub integration.

tools.name: LLM Foundry
tools.description: LLM Foundry is MosaicML's codebase for training, fine-tuning, evaluating, and exporting language models using Composer, with configuration-driven recipes and distributed strategies such as FSDP.

tools.name: LitGPT
tools.description: LitGPT is Lightning AI's readable implementation and training toolkit for many language-model architectures, with pretraining, full fine-tuning, LoRA/QLoRA, adapter, quantization, and checkpoint-conversion recipes.

tools.name: torchtune
tools.description: torchtune is a PyTorch-native post-training library organized around hackable recipes for SFT, LoRA/QLoRA, knowledge distillation, preference optimization, quantization-aware training, and distributed execution.

tools.name: Hugging Face Transformers
tools.description: Hugging Face Transformers provides model implementations, tokenizers, data collators, generation utilities, and the Trainer API used by many SFT and post-training stacks across PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX.

tools.name: MosaicML Composer
tools.description: MosaicML Composer is a PyTorch training library with a configurable Trainer, distributed execution, checkpointing, callbacks, and algorithm hooks; it is the training engine beneath LLM Foundry.

tools.name: MLX LM
tools.description: MLX LM is Apple's toolkit for generating from and fine-tuning language models with MLX on Apple silicon, including full, LoRA, and QLoRA-style training plus model conversion.

tools.name: MaxText
tools.description: MaxText is a JAX reference implementation for scalable transformer training on Google Cloud accelerators, with pretraining and fine-tuning configurations, distributed sharding, checkpointing, and performance recipes.

tools.name: EasyLM
tools.description: EasyLM is a JAX/Flax language-model training codebase with scripts for pretraining, supervised fine-tuning, evaluation, and checkpoint conversion across supported transformer architectures.

tools.name: OLMo-core
tools.description: OLMo-core is AI2's PyTorch library for building and training language models, exposing distributed training, data mixing, checkpointing, optimizers, and reusable components from the OLMo program.

tools.name: Nanotron
tools.description: Nanotron is Hugging Face's minimal distributed transformer-training library for pretraining and fine-tuning, with tensor, pipeline, data, context, and expert parallelism.

tools.name: PyTorch Lightning
tools.description: PyTorch Lightning is a structured training-loop framework that supplies distributed strategies, mixed precision, callbacks, logging, checkpointing, and accelerator portability for custom language-model fine-tuning code.

tools.name: KerasHub
tools.description: KerasHub is the Keras library of pretrained modeling components and task APIs, including causal-language-model presets, LoRA enablement, tokenization, generation, and fine-tuning across supported backends.

tools.name: Hugging Face PEFT
tools.description: Hugging Face PEFT is a library for training, composing, merging, saving, and loading parameter-efficient adapters, including LoRA, DoRA, AdaLoRA, IA3, prompt tuning, prefix tuning, and related methods.

tools.name: loralib
tools.description: loralib is Microsoft's reference PyTorch implementation accompanying the LoRA paper, providing low-rank replacements for common layers and utilities for marking only LoRA parameters trainable.

tools.name: Adapters
tools.description: Adapters is a unified library for parameter-efficient and modular transfer learning, integrating LoRA, prefix tuning, bottleneck adapters, composition, merging, and quantized adapter training with transformer models.

tools.name: AdapterHub
tools.description: AdapterHub is an open ecosystem for discovering, sharing, and composing pretrained adapter modules; each uploaded adapter remains governed by its own metadata, base-model license, and usage terms.

tools.name: OpenDelta
tools.description: OpenDelta is a plug-and-play delta-tuning library that injects trainable LoRA, prefix, adapter, and other lightweight modules into supported pretrained PyTorch models.

tools.name: QLoRA
tools.description: QLoRA is the reference implementation for fine-tuning LoRA adapters over a frozen 4-bit quantized base model using NF4, double quantization, and paged optimizers.

tools.name: mergekit
tools.description: mergekit is an Apache-licensed toolkit for combining language-model checkpoints and LoRA adapters with multiple merge methods, out-of-core processing, tensor selection, and configuration-driven workflows.

tools.name: LLM-Adapters
tools.description: LLM-Adapters is a research toolkit and benchmark for applying and comparing parameter-efficient adapters across language-model tasks, including LoRA-family and bottleneck-style methods.

tools.name: LoRA-GA
tools.description: LoRA-GA is the reference implementation of gradient-approximation initialization for LoRA adapters, intended to initialize low-rank updates using estimated full fine-tuning gradients.

tools.name: DeepSpeed
tools.description: DeepSpeed is Microsoft's distributed deep-learning library providing ZeRO optimizer-state and parameter sharding, pipeline and tensor parallelism, mixed precision, offload, checkpointing, and training optimizations used by many LLM frameworks.

tools.name: Megatron-LM
tools.description: Megatron-LM is NVIDIA's framework for large-scale transformer training with tensor, pipeline, sequence, context, data, and expert parallelism plus distributed checkpointing.

tools.name: ColossalAI
tools.description: ColossalAI is a distributed training system with sharded optimizers, heterogeneous memory management, tensor and pipeline parallelism, mixture-of-experts support, and LLM fine-tuning examples.

tools.name: Hugging Face Accelerate
tools.description: Hugging Face Accelerate provides a unified interface for running PyTorch training code across CPUs, GPUs, TPUs, mixed precision, DeepSpeed, FSDP, and multi-node configurations.

tools.name: PyTorch FSDP
tools.description: PyTorch FSDP is PyTorch's native Fully Sharded Data Parallel API for sharding model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across data-parallel workers during training.

tools.name: TorchTitan
tools.description: TorchTitan is a PyTorch-native reference platform for large-model training that composes FSDP, tensor, pipeline, context, and expert parallelism with distributed checkpointing and profiling.

tools.name: Ray Train
tools.description: Ray Train is Ray's distributed training library for scaling PyTorch and Hugging Face workloads across workers and clusters with fault tolerance, storage integration, and orchestration APIs.

tools.name: NVIDIA Transformer Engine
tools.description: NVIDIA Transformer Engine provides optimized transformer layers, attention, and mixed-precision formats such as FP8 for training on supported NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel accelerators.

tools.name: FlashAttention
tools.description: FlashAttention is an exact, IO-aware attention implementation with CUDA kernels commonly integrated into fine-tuning frameworks to reduce attention memory traffic and support longer sequences.

tools.name: Liger Kernel
tools.description: Liger Kernel is a collection of Triton kernels for transformer training operations, including fused losses, normalization, rotary embeddings, and memory-efficient linear cross entropy.

tools.name: xFormers
tools.description: xFormers is Meta's library of composable and optimized transformer building blocks, including memory-efficient attention operators used by training and fine-tuning systems.

tools.name: Hugging Face TRL
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: Hugging Face TRL is a post-training library built on Transformers with trainers for SFT, reward modeling, DPO, KTO, GRPO, and related algorithms, plus PEFT and distributed-backend integrations.

tools.name: OpenRLHF
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: OpenRLHF is a Ray- and vLLM-based post-training framework for SFT, reward models, DPO-family methods, PPO, GRPO, REINFORCE variants, and asynchronous or colocated rollout architectures.

tools.name: veRL
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: veRL is an open reinforcement-learning framework using HybridFlow to compose training and rollout backends, with PPO, GRPO, DAPO, agentic rollouts, FSDP, Megatron, vLLM, and SGLang integrations.

tools.name: NVIDIA NeMo RL
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: NVIDIA NeMo RL is a scalable post-training library for language and vision-language models, covering SFT, DPO, GRPO-family reinforcement learning, distributed execution, rollout backends, and LoRA.

tools.name: Open-Instruct
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: Open-Instruct is AI2's codebase of reproducible recipes for instruction tuning, preference optimization, reward modeling, and reinforcement learning used in the Tülu model program.

tools.name: Alignment Handbook
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: Alignment Handbook is Hugging Face's collection of recipes and educational material for continued pretraining, SFT, reward modeling, DPO, and model evaluation with the Transformers stack.

tools.name: AReaL
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: AReaL is a large-scale asynchronous reinforcement-learning system with separate training, inference, agent, and weight-update services for reasoning and agentic model post-training.

tools.name: SkyRL
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: SkyRL is a modular full-stack reinforcement-learning library supporting training, sampling, environments, long-horizon agents, and colocated or disaggregated execution across multiple model backends.

tools.name: slime
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: slime is an LLM reinforcement-learning scaling framework that connects Megatron training with SGLang rollouts and supports synchronous, asynchronous, dense, and mixture-of-experts post-training workflows.

tools.name: RAGEN
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: RAGEN is a framework for reinforcement-learning training and diagnosis of multi-turn reasoning agents in interactive environments using trajectory- and turn-level reward structures.

tools.name: DeepSpeed-Chat
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: DeepSpeed-Chat is a reference RLHF pipeline in DeepSpeedExamples that covers SFT, reward-model training, and PPO-based actor–critic fine-tuning with ZeRO-powered distributed execution.

tools.name: ROLL
tools.category: RLHF, Preference Optimization, and Reinforcement Learning
tools.description: ROLL is Alibaba's reinforcement-learning library for large models, providing modular training and rollout components for reasoning, agentic, multimodal, and preference-optimization workloads.

tools.name: Argilla
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Argilla is a collaborative data platform for curating, annotating, and reviewing language-model datasets, including response rating, ranking, classification, and human-feedback workflows.

tools.name: Label Studio
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Label Studio is an open-source data-labeling interface with commercial enterprise features, supporting text, chat, ranking, audio, image, and multimodal annotation projects.

tools.name: NeMo Curator
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: NeMo Curator is NVIDIA's scalable toolkit for downloading, extracting, deduplicating, filtering, classifying, and generating text, image, video, and synthetic datasets for model training.

tools.name: Data-Juicer
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Data-Juicer is ModelScope's system for processing, filtering, deduplicating, mixing, and analyzing text and multimodal datasets with configurable operators and distributed execution.

tools.name: DataTrove
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: DataTrove is Hugging Face's library for large-scale text data processing with readers, writers, filters, deduplication, tokenization, statistics, and local or cluster execution.

tools.name: Dolma Toolkit
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Dolma Toolkit is AI2's toolkit for curating language-model pretraining corpora through document processing, filtering, deduplication, mixing, and tokenization pipelines.

tools.name: Cleanlab
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Cleanlab is a data-centric machine-learning library for detecting label errors, outliers, near duplicates, and low-quality examples that can contaminate fine-tuning datasets.

tools.name: Prodigy
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Prodigy is a scriptable annotation tool from the spaCy team with model-in-the-loop recipes for text classification, named entities, preference collection, and custom interfaces.

tools.name: Labelbox
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Labelbox is a commercial data platform for annotation, curation, and managed human evaluation across text and multimodal data, including preference and response-quality projects.

tools.name: Scale Data Engine
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Scale Data Engine is a managed data-labeling and curation service for collecting expert demonstrations, preferences, evaluations, and multimodal training data.

tools.name: Snorkel Flow
tools.category: Data Curation, Labeling, and Human Feedback
tools.description: Snorkel Flow is a commercial data-development platform for programmatic labeling, data curation, model iteration, and subject-matter-expert workflows, derived from the Snorkel research project.

tools.name: distilabel
tools.description: distilabel is an Argilla framework for building reproducible synthetic-data and AI-feedback pipelines, with task abstractions, model-provider integrations, batching, caching, and dataset export.

tools.name: DataDreamer
tools.description: DataDreamer is a Python library for reproducible synthetic-data generation and training workflows with cached steps, prompt and embedding providers, dataset operations, and Hugging Face integrations.

tools.name: Synthetic Data Kit
tools.description: Synthetic Data Kit is Meta's command-line and library workflow for ingesting documents, generating and curating question–answer or reasoning examples, and preparing fine-tuning datasets.

tools.name: Augmentoolkit
tools.description: Augmentoolkit is a configurable pipeline for converting source documents into synthetic instruction, conversation, and domain datasets using local or API-hosted language models.

tools.name: Bonito
tools.description: Bonito is an open task-generation model and codebase for converting unannotated text into task-specific instruction-tuning examples through conditional task generation.

tools.name: Self-Instruct
tools.description: Self-Instruct is the reference code and data-generation pipeline for bootstrapping instruction-following datasets by generating, filtering, and instantiating new tasks from seed instructions.

tools.name: LLM2LLM
tools.description: LLM2LLM is a reference implementation for iterative data augmentation that uses a stronger model to generate corrective examples from a smaller model's errors.

tools.name: Prompt2Model
tools.description: Prompt2Model is a research system that turns a natural-language task description into a small specialized model by retrieving data, generating synthetic examples, training, and evaluating.

tools.name: Gretel
tools.description: Gretel is a commercial synthetic-data platform for generating and transforming text, tabular, and structured datasets with privacy and quality controls.

tools.name: Mostly AI
tools.description: Mostly AI provides an open-source synthetic-data SDK and a commercial platform for generating privacy-preserving tabular and sequential data that can supplement model-training workflows.

tools.name: Weights & Biases
tools.description: Weights & Biases provides experiment tracking, dashboards, artifact and model registries, reports, and sweep orchestration through open-source clients and a hosted or self-managed platform.

tools.name: MLflow
tools.description: MLflow is an open-source platform for experiment tracking, model packaging, evaluation, registry workflows, and deployment metadata across machine-learning and generative-AI projects.

tools.name: Comet
tools.description: Comet provides experiment tracking, model registry, artifact management, panels, and production monitoring through open SDKs and a commercial platform.

tools.name: ClearML
tools.description: ClearML is an open-source experiment manager and orchestration suite with dataset versioning, agents, pipelines, artifact storage, model registry, and commercial enterprise services.

tools.name: Aim
tools.description: Aim is a self-hostable experiment tracker for logging, querying, comparing, and visualizing metrics, parameters, distributions, text, and training artifacts.

tools.name: TensorBoard
tools.description: TensorBoard is an open-source visualization toolkit for training metrics, computational graphs, embeddings, profiling, and experiment comparison through event logs.

tools.name: DVCLive
tools.description: DVCLive is Iterative's experiment-logging library for metrics, parameters, plots, and artifacts, designed to connect training code with DVC experiments and Studio.

tools.name: Trackio
tools.description: Trackio is a lightweight local experiment tracker with a Python logging API and dashboard, designed to integrate with Hugging Face training workflows and Spaces.

tools.name: Ray Tune
tools.description: Ray Tune is a distributed hyperparameter-tuning library with search algorithms, schedulers, early stopping, checkpoint handling, and integrations for PyTorch and Hugging Face trainers.

tools.name: Optuna
tools.description: Optuna is a framework-agnostic hyperparameter-optimization library with define-by-run search spaces, samplers, pruning, distributed storage, visualization, and experiment analysis.

tools.name: Hydra
tools.description: Hydra is Meta's hierarchical configuration framework for composing training settings and launching local or distributed multirun sweeps through configurable launchers and sweepers.

tools.name: Ax
tools.description: Ax is Meta's adaptive experimentation platform for Bayesian optimization and multi-objective search, with service and developer APIs for iterative training experiments.

tools.name: Katib
tools.description: Katib is Kubeflow's Kubernetes-native system for hyperparameter tuning, early stopping, and neural-architecture search using jobs and pluggable suggestion algorithms.

tools.name: NNI
tools.description: NNI is Microsoft's AutoML toolkit for running hyperparameter search, pruning, neural-architecture search, and model-compression experiments on local or remote training services.

tools.name: Nevergrad
tools.description: Nevergrad is Meta's derivative-free optimization library with evolutionary, bandit, and population-based optimizers suitable for noisy or non-differentiable training objectives.

tools.name: Determined AI
tools.description: Determined AI is an open-source distributed training and hyperparameter-search platform with scheduling, fault tolerance, checkpointing, experiment tracking, and HPE enterprise offerings.

tools.name: bitsandbytes
tools.description: bitsandbytes provides low-bit linear layers, 8-bit optimizers, and 4-bit NF4/FP4 loading used by QLoRA and other memory-efficient fine-tuning workflows across supported hardware.

tools.name: GPTQModel
tools.description: GPTQModel is the actively maintained successor to AutoGPTQ for GPTQ and related model quantization, with Transformers, vLLM, and SGLang integrations across multiple accelerator and CPU backends.

tools.name: LLM Compressor
tools.description: LLM Compressor is the vLLM project's library for applying GPTQ, AWQ, SmoothQuant, AutoRound, FP8, FP4, and other compression schemes and saving compatible compressed-tensors checkpoints.

tools.name: AWQ
tools.description: AWQ is the official implementation of Activation-aware Weight Quantization, a post-training weight-only method that protects salient channels using activation statistics and calibrated scaling.

tools.name: HQQ
tools.description: HQQ is a half-quadratic quantization library for on-the-fly low-bit model quantization and dequantization, with backend integrations for Transformers and several inference engines.

tools.name: torchao
tools.description: torchao is PyTorch's native quantization and sparsity library for training and inference, including low-bit dtypes, quantization-aware training, post-training quantization, and model transformations.

tools.name: Quanto
tools.description: Quanto is Hugging Face's PyTorch quantization toolkit for weights and activations, supporting calibration, quantization-aware training, freezing, serialization, and device-independent model loading.

tools.name: Intel Neural Compressor
tools.description: Intel Neural Compressor is a cross-framework optimization toolkit for post-training quantization, quantization-aware training, pruning, distillation, and model export on supported Intel hardware.

tools.name: TensorRT Model Optimizer
tools.description: TensorRT Model Optimizer is NVIDIA's library for quantization, sparsity, distillation, speculative decoding, and checkpoint optimization before deployment through TensorRT-LLM or related runtimes.

tools.name: AQLM
tools.description: AQLM is the reference implementation of additive quantization for compressing language-model weights with learned codebooks and fine-tuning the resulting quantized representation.

tools.name: QuIP#
tools.description: QuIP# is a research implementation of lattice-codebook and incoherence-processing methods for very-low-bit post-training quantization of language models.

tools.name: SmoothQuant
tools.description: SmoothQuant is the official implementation of a training-free method that migrates activation outliers into weights to enable W8A8 quantization of transformer models.

tools.name: AutoRound
tools.description: AutoRound is Intel's weight-rounding quantization toolkit for language and multimodal models, with low-bit calibration, multiple export formats, and serving-framework integrations.

tools.name: llama.cpp
tools.description: llama.cpp provides model-conversion scripts, GGUF quantization tools, adapter loading, and CPU/GPU inference for supported architectures, making it a common deployment target for merged fine-tuned checkpoints.

tools.name: vLLM
tools.description: vLLM is a high-throughput model-serving engine with OpenAI-compatible APIs, distributed inference, quantized checkpoint support, and runtime loading of supported LoRA adapters.

tools.name: SGLang
tools.description: SGLang is a serving and programming framework for language and multimodal models with distributed inference, structured generation, quantization, and LoRA adapter support.

tools.name: TensorRT-LLM
tools.description: TensorRT-LLM is NVIDIA's inference library and compiler stack for optimizing, converting, quantizing, and serving supported language-model checkpoints on NVIDIA GPUs.

tools.name: Hugging Face Optimum
tools.description: Hugging Face Optimum provides export and hardware-optimization interfaces connecting Transformers models to ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, and other accelerator-specific backends.

tools.name: ONNX Runtime GenAI
tools.description: ONNX Runtime GenAI supplies generation APIs, model builders, quantization utilities, and runtime support for deploying converted generative-model checkpoints through ONNX Runtime.

tools.name: MLC LLM
tools.description: MLC LLM compiles and packages language models for deployment across GPUs, CPUs, browsers, and mobile devices, with conversion, quantization, and OpenAI-compatible serving tools.

tools.name: ExecuTorch
tools.description: ExecuTorch is PyTorch's on-device inference stack for exporting, optimizing, and running supported models on mobile and embedded hardware through backend delegates.

tools.name: LMDeploy
tools.description: LMDeploy is an inference and compression toolkit for language and vision-language models, with TurboMind and PyTorch engines, quantization, serving APIs, and adapter support.

tools.name: OpenVINO GenAI
tools.description: OpenVINO GenAI provides generation pipelines, model conversion, compression integrations, and serving examples for running supported language and multimodal models on Intel hardware.

tools.name: LoRAX
tools.description: LoRAX is an Apache-licensed multi-adapter inference server that dynamically loads LoRA adapters and exposes OpenAI-compatible APIs, Kubernetes assets, metrics, and tenant isolation.

tools.name: Together AI Fine-Tuning
tools.description: Together AI Fine-Tuning runs LoRA or full-parameter jobs on supported open-weight base models and connects completed models to dedicated hosted endpoints, with validation and checkpoint options documented per model.

tools.name: Fireworks AI Fine-Tuning
tools.description: Fireworks AI Fine-Tuning provides managed SFT, DPO, and reinforcement fine-tuning for supported models through its UI, CLI, and API, with model-weight download and dedicated deployment options.

tools.name: Predibase
tools.description: Predibase is a managed platform for fine-tuning and serving open-weight models with LoRA and QLoRA, built by the team behind the separate open-source Ludwig and LoRAX projects.

tools.name: OpenPipe
tools.description: OpenPipe is a managed platform that collects and curates application traces into training datasets, runs supported fine-tuning jobs, evaluates resulting models, and serves them through an API.

tools.name: Tinker
tools.description: Tinker is Thinking Machines Lab's managed training API for LoRA-based SFT and reinforcement learning on supported open-weight models, exposing gradient, optimizer, sampling, and checkpoint primitives while operating distributed infrastructure.

tools.name: OpenAI Fine-Tuning
tools.description: OpenAI Fine-Tuning is a managed API and dashboard workflow for SFT, DPO, and reinforcement fine-tuning on selected OpenAI models, with uploaded datasets, job events, checkpoints, and hosted inference.

tools.name: Vertex AI Model Tuning
tools.description: Vertex AI Model Tuning provides managed supervised and preference tuning for selected Google and open models, with training data in Google Cloud and deployment through Vertex AI endpoints.

tools.name: Amazon Bedrock Model Customization
tools.description: Amazon Bedrock Model Customization provides managed fine-tuning, continued pretraining, distillation, model import, and reinforcement fine-tuning capabilities for supported foundation models and regions.

tools.name: Microsoft Foundry Fine-Tuning
tools.description: Microsoft Foundry Fine-Tuning provides managed SFT, DPO, and reinforcement fine-tuning for supported OpenAI and selected open models, with region- and deployment-specific availability.

tools.name: Amazon SageMaker AI Fine-Tuning
tools.description: Amazon SageMaker AI provides configurable foundation-model fine-tuning through JumpStart, training jobs, distributed libraries, managed storage, experiment tracking, and endpoint deployment.

tools.name: Databricks AI Runtime
tools.description: Databricks AI Runtime is a serverless GPU environment for custom training and fine-tuning with frameworks such as PyTorch, Transformers, Unsloth, and LLM Foundry; some distributed capabilities remain preview or beta.

tools.name: IBM watsonx.ai Fine-Tuning
tools.description: IBM watsonx.ai provides LoRA and QLoRA fine-tuning for supported foundation models and regions, alongside tuning experiments, deployment, and governance features; supported methods vary by product edition.

tools.name: Cohere Fine-Tuning
tools.description: Cohere Fine-Tuning trains and deploys supported Cohere models from uploaded datasets through its API and dashboard; model eligibility and retirement dates are documented in Cohere's current model lifecycle pages.

tools.name: Baseten Training
tools.description: Baseten Training runs custom training containers on managed GPUs and deploys produced checkpoints; its separate Loops early-access service exposes Tinker-compatible LoRA SFT and reinforcement-learning primitives.

tools.name: Lamini
tools.description: Lamini provides managed and self-hosted enterprise APIs for tuning supported open-weight models, including conventional fine-tuning and its adapter-based Memory Tuning workflow.

tools.name: DeepEval
tools.description: DeepEval is an open-source evaluation framework for regression-testing fine-tuned checkpoints with versioned test cases, custom or built-in metrics, benchmark runners, and CI integration.

tools.name: lm-evaluation-harness
tools.description: lm-evaluation-harness is EleutherAI's benchmark runner for comparing language-model checkpoints across a large registry of academic tasks, prompting configurations, and inference backends.

tools.name: LightEval
tools.description: LightEval is Hugging Face's toolkit for evaluating language models across local, distributed, and hosted backends with configurable tasks, metrics, prompt formats, and result tracking.

tools.name: OpenCompass
tools.description: OpenCompass is an evaluation platform for language and multimodal checkpoints with dataset registries, distributed inference, judge-based and objective metrics, and report generation.

tools.name: Inspect AI
tools.description: Inspect AI is the UK AI Security Institute's evaluation framework for defining datasets, solvers, scorers, model backends, sandboxed tool use, and reproducible evaluation logs.

tools.name: EvalScope
tools.description: EvalScope is ModelScope's framework for evaluating and profiling language, multimodal, embedding, and reranker models across benchmark and inference backends.

tools.name: RewardBench
tools.description: RewardBench is AI2's benchmark and codebase for comparing reward-model checkpoints on preference pairs, helping select reward models before RLHF or rejection-sampling pipelines.

tools.name: AlpacaEval
tools.description: AlpacaEval is an automatic instruction-following evaluator for pairwise comparison of model outputs, with length-controlled variants and reproducible leaderboard tooling.

tools.name: OLMES
tools.description: OLMES is AI2's standardized evaluation framework and reporting protocol for reproducibly comparing base and instruction-tuned language-model checkpoints.

tools.name: FinGPT
tools.description: FinGPT is an open research framework for financial language models with data pipelines, instruction-tuning and LoRA scripts, sentiment and forecasting tasks, and published model recipes.

tools.name: NVIDIA BioNeMo Framework
tools.description: NVIDIA BioNeMo Framework provides domain-specific libraries and scalable recipes for training and LoRA-tuning biomolecular foundation models such as protein and DNA sequence models.

tools.name: AdaptLLM
tools.description: AdaptLLM is Microsoft's reference code for reading-comprehension-based domain adaptation, converting raw domain corpora into training tasks for continued pretraining and instruction tuning.

tools.name: LLaMA-Adapter
tools.description: LLaMA-Adapter is a research codebase for parameter-efficient instruction and multimodal adaptation using small trainable modules; released checkpoints remain subject to their underlying model licenses.

tools.name: LAVIS
tools.description: LAVIS is Salesforce's library for training and evaluating language–vision models, with standardized datasets, processors, tasks, model implementations, and parameter-efficient adaptation recipes.

tools.name: MedicalGPT
tools.description: MedicalGPT is a training toolkit with continued pretraining, SFT, reward modeling, DPO, and PPO scripts for medical-domain language models; generated checkpoints inherit their base-model terms.

tools.name: FinLoRA
tools.description: FinLoRA publishes financial LoRA adapter checkpoints and reproducible training scripts; its downloadable adapters remain subject to the repository's terms plus underlying base-model and dataset licenses.

tools.name: AutoGPTQ
tools.description: AutoGPTQ is archived and unmaintained; its maintainers direct new GPTQ work to GPTQModel, and current Transformers releases no longer support AutoGPTQ.

tools.name: AutoAWQ
tools.description: AutoAWQ is archived and officially deprecated; its repository directs new AWQ quantization work to the vLLM project's LLM Compressor.

tools.name: TRLX
tools.description: TRLX is an early distributed RLHF framework for PPO and ILQL whose repository is not archived but has received no code push since January 2024.

tools.name: Stanford Alpaca
tools.description: Stanford Alpaca is the static 2023 reference project for Self-Instruct-style data generation and LLaMA fine-tuning; its released model artifacts were governed by the original LLaMA license.

tools.name: Alpaca-LoRA
tools.description: Alpaca-LoRA is an influential early LoRA fine-tuning example for LLaMA whose implementation is retained primarily as a historical reference for the 2023 ecosystem.

tools.name: RL4LMs
tools.description: RL4LMs is AI2's modular reinforcement-learning library for language generation; its archived code remains useful for reproducing PPO and related research baselines.

tools.name: OpenAssistant
tools.description: OpenAssistant is LAION's concluded project for crowdsourcing assistant conversations, reward labels, and open training recipes; the repository and datasets remain available for research.

tools.name: OpenChatKit
tools.description: OpenChatKit is Together's 2023 toolkit of chat-model training, moderation, retrieval, and evaluation code; development has slowed and current Together fine-tuning is offered separately as a managed service.

tools.name: adapter-transformers
tools.description: adapter-transformers is the superseded predecessor to the standalone Adapters library; AdapterHub directs new development and migrations to Adapters.

tools.name: GPTQ-for-LLaMa
tools.description: GPTQ-for-LLaMa is an early GPTQ implementation retained for historical reproducibility; current quantization stacks provide broader model, kernel, serialization, and hardware support.
